title: Billionaire Tech Investor Says $15,000,000,000,000 US Labor Market ‘Would Mostly Go Away’ As AI Drives Massive Deflation
author: Secure_Persimmon8369
selftext: Famed billionaire tech investor Vinod Khosla believes that the US economy will witness a massive transformation in the coming years as AI eventually performs the majority of human jobs.

comments.author: Secure_Persimmon8369
comments.body: Khosla really said the $15T labor market is 404-ing soon. AI deflation is great for our tech stack costs, but scary for our revenue models. As small biz owners, are we leaning into the 'abundance' or are we worried the deflation will outpace our ability to pivot? Let's talk strategy. 📉🤖

comments.author: Aromatic-Fishing9952
comments.body:
Ah yes replace all of our workforce with machines. Leave unskilled humans. Then the sun decides to shoot its load at us and then we are really fucked as everyone is dumb as a caveman without Claude or GPT.

How about the rich stop trying to make everyone else irrelevant? AI doesn’t mean we have people do less, it means we let people be more efficient and do more with the tools. If we can let AI augment our work flow, we don’t need to work as much. We get to do the fun stuff, and let the AI handle the bullshit copypasta.

replies.author: StreamSpaces
replies.body: How does AI lead to people being dumb? It’s a personal choice how you spend your life.

replies.author: Aromatic-Fishing9952
replies.body:
Skills and knowledge are built with an objective. If there is no economic grant that allows you to build those skills, people will not learn them.

If AI does everything then people won’t have these opportunities to learn and grow. Entry level people need entry level challenges. Hence people will get dumber.

replies.author: StreamSpaces
replies.body: Ahh the economics of learning. Got ya.

replies.author: West-Following2465
replies.body:
This is like saying we need manual labor with treadmills to produce electricity. Imo, anything that we can automate, the better, we get more consistent results and less interaction. We can spend our time more productively advancing our minds and learning how to innovate even further. I am unemployed and have several projects that im currently engaged in and am more productive than ever with AI. We need to let big businesses have their automation and stop being their "human resources."" We are fucking humans that have been duped into thinking a corporate job is the ticket to success. Ive got news for you, its a fucking trap. Automation and AI are going to eliminate modern-day day slavery. The only question is, "Can you be your own boss?" and utilize the powerful resources at your disposal to enrich yourself with financial earnings? 

Will it displace a lot of people? Absolutely. Survival of the fittest. Im using my time wisely, and financial earnings are just about automated where im earning money without work. I built a day trading cryptocurrency system that generates $-$150.00 a day on the lowest day (1 out of 15 days aren't profitable) and earns a solid living off the system. I got in early, just finishing development this week.

replies.author: Aromatic-Fishing9952
replies.body:
You missed the point big time. The ramifications of replacing human labor en masse with AGI is massive and will destroy our current economic system.

You are using AI as a tool. Awesome. You magically built a system that beats the best traders in the world on average return with a an over 10x R/R on crypto markets. Let us know when your hedge fund is open. 

Ai is a tool. But they don’t want a tool. They want to replace the average worker. And not every family would be able to trade crypto with an automated bot to make money.

replies.author: West-Following2465
replies.body: Wow. I feel absolutely ashamed of my lack of relatively. I'm completely bias towards AI. My bad. Ill leave my previous post for entertainment. You're right.

replies.author: West-Following2465
replies.body: I want to note that the system took me over 7 months of 12 hour days, 7 days a week until it went live. It wasn't an easy win.

replies.author: Aromatic-Fishing9952
replies.body: That’s cool. I’ve been working on a trading system for 5 years, and even then my R/R is on average slightly less than 2.

replies.author: West-Following2465
replies.body: The money is actually in memecoins at the moment. I built a system with 42 machine learning systems that handle all of the trade decisions. I have AI monitoring the hold times. Machine learning is a huge factor in the success of this system. It was a bitch getting them all working, trained, and then not argue and work together. Overall, the project was fun, and I consider it my legacy.

replies.author: West-Following2465
replies.body: I have a 27.1% win rate with $44.00/$100.00 profit per dollar spent. The win rate is was initially discouraging, but seeing those occasional huge wins (500%-4000% PnL). I came to the realization that this is the real world and the win rate doesnt matter. It brings in money, which is the bottom line.

comments.author: Revolutionary_Sir352
comments.body:
His last name sounds Iike “Coleslaw” every time I read it. This helps me avoid being concerned.

I do think AI will reduce # of jobs by 50% or more overall in the very long term (30 years) so yeah I think save all ur money is all you can do. 

There definitely won’t be deflation IMO. Govt will print money so there will be inflation before allowing tons of people to starve. 

There might be very brief periods of insanely high deflation between all the inflationary periods but overall $100 USD will buy like 5 bananas in 30 years 

There would be riots in the streets and the people would redistribute the means of production.

Source: I’m not that smart so I will most likely be wrong except for the $100 = 5 bananas. I’m pretty sure that’s gonna happen

comments.author: Ok_Neighborhood6782
comments.body: Ai will only deflate the stock market

comments.author: Background-Farm-2320
comments.body: I’m in pharma sales. They have been trying to replace us for 30 years (including with AI). It doesn’t work because humans communicate ideas and emotions with each other. Not sure how AI is going to replace that. Regarding professionals like drs and lawyers sure AI can do the technical stuff but the other 50% is being reassured by a human being.

replies.author: Substantial_Sound272
replies.body: Easy. AI doctors and AI patients.

comments.author: Recent_Strawberry456
comments.body: Newly unemployed? I’ve got just the thing: a ground-floor opportunity in the military featuring foreign travel, new experiences, and the occasional person shooting at you. Honestly, it’s basically tourism with higher stakes.  Face it, what else is there?

comments.author: Daveonaltair4
comments.body: This is ridiculous. We have a goods and services economy, who's going to be able to pay for all the goods and services? Is basically 95% of the population just going to starve in the fields like the irish potato famine while a small fraction of tech owners and tech billionaires hide in their walled castles? How's this supposed to work?

comments.author: Ok-Replacement9595
comments.body: Man, I hope we get deflation.

comments.author: Nerdymcbutthead
comments.body:
The rich elite lay everyone off. No one’s has jobs to purchase the rich elite products.  
Economy collapses, rich elite share portfolio collapses!!

comments.author: OrvilleTheCavalier
comments.body: Who do they think is going to buy their products if there is no labor force earning money to buy products?

comments.author: rindor1990
comments.body: Mhmmm, AI gonna wipe my ass too?

comments.author: BackgroundResult
comments.body: Here is a great breakdown of the actual situation regarding this paper, AI and the real labor market in the U.S: [https://www.ai-supremacy.com/p/the-us-labor-market-and-its-ai-problem-2026](https://www.ai-supremacy.com/p/the-us-labor-market-and-its-ai-problem-2026)
